challenge_text: Open subscribers until something breaks. Honestly report the CONTAINER ceiling and the limiting resource (the real target is a $5 Pi, but only the Linux container is available), and whether DEMO names the right limiting resource (RAM, ~2.4MB/sub).
capabilities_attempted_but_failed: reaching an actual RAM/fd/process wall in the container (8GB box never ran out at 250 subs; could not observe the silent-degradation failure mode this challenge wants to characterize)

friction_points.description: DEMO 'Measured numbers' quotes per-sub RSS-flavored framing ('~5 procs + ~2.4MB RAM per sub') but RSS double-counts shared library/binary pages: ps RSS sum reports ~14.4MB/sub at 50-250 subs, while the honest sharing-aware MemAvailable delta is ~2.39MB/sub (2386 kB measured at 200 subs from a clean idle baseline). The ~2.4MB figure is correct, but a Pi user running 'ps -o rss' for capacity planning will see ~14MB/sub and panic. DEMO should name MemAvailable-delta as the measurement method so the user does not mis-plan from RSS.
friction_points.workaround: Measure free RAM before/after (MemAvailable delta), not summed RSS.

friction_points.description: Observability gap at the memory ceiling persists (this is the round-001 HIGH 'silent-fanout-failure-under-mem-pressure', listed as punted H5). I could not trigger it in the 8GB container (every functional probe from 10 to 250 subs delivered: got='x-250' etc.), so I cannot confirm or refute the silent-failure behavior on a real 512MB Pi. The Pi user's real risk - a sub that registers a FIFO but whose handler was OOM-killed so it silently never delivers - has no health/liveness counter to detect it (only sub_*.fifo presence, which does not prove the handler lives). Documented as a non-goal, but a Pi operator still has no black-box signal.
friction_points.workaround: None observable; user must externally OOM-monitor and not trust fifo-count as liveness.

wished_for: DEMO to state the measurement method (MemAvailable delta, not summed RSS) next to the 2.4MB/sub number

a per-topic live-subscriber liveness signal that proves the handler/drainer behind each sub_*.fifo is actually alive (not just that the FIFO exists), so the RAM-ceiling failure mode is observable on a Pi
a one-line 'measure on your box' helper (e.g. a small tests/ceiling.sh) so the Pi user gets their own number without hand-rolling /proc accounting
what_worked_well: Per-sub RAM measured at 2386 kB via MemAvailable delta - near-exact match to DEMO's claimed ~2.4MB/sub; DEMO names the RIGHT limiting resource (RAM)
fds and processes are demonstrably NOT the wall: broker held a flat 4 fds and 3 direct children from 10 to 250 subs while RAM climbed linearly - exactly what DEMO claims
linear, predictable scaling: ~5 total procs/sub, ~2.4MB/sub, no knees or cliffs up to 250 subs; capacity planning is trustworthy
0.000% idle CPU across the whole broker fleet with 30 idle subscribers over a 4s jiffy sample - the persona's core idle-power requirement is met
every functional probe (10/25/50/75/100/150/200/250 subs) delivered correctly - no degradation observed within container RAM
comparison_to_expectations: Round-001 scored 7/10 with the chief complaint being DEMO mis-naming the ceiling resource (procs) and giving no per-sub cost for Pi planning. Both are now fixed: DEMO names RAM and quotes ~2.4MB/sub and ~100-150 subs on a 512MB Pi, and my live measurement (2386 kB/sub) confirms it almost exactly. That is a real, verifiable improvement. The only residual is the punted observability gap at the wall, which I could not exercise in an 8GB container.
free_text: Container ceiling: I never hit a hard wall - 8GB box delivered to 250 concurrent subs with fds flat (4) and broker children flat (3). The limiting resource is unambiguously RAM: ~2.39MB/sub (MemAvailable delta, 200 subs from clean idle baseline), linear. On a 512MB Pi that puts the wall at roughly 100-160 subs after OS+broker overhead, which matches DEMO's stated ~100-150. VERDICT: DEMO honestly states the wall and names the right limiting resource (RAM, not fd/process). One nit: the ~2.4MB number is the sharing-aware figure; a user measuring summed ps RSS sees ~14MB/sub (shared pages double-counted) - DEMO should say which method it used. The round-001 HIGH about silent fan-out failure under memory pressure is documented as a non-goal (H5) but could not be reproduced here (no OOM in 8GB), so it stays a real residual risk for the actual Pi, not something I can clear.

claim_audit.claim: The subscriber ceiling is RAM-bound (~2.4 MB/sub: socat + handler + drainer), not fd- or pid-bound (the broker holds only a handful of fds)
claim_audit.evidence: MemAvailable delta = 2386 kB/sub at 200 subs from clean idle baseline; broker /proc/<pid>/fd held flat at 4, direct children flat at 3, from 10 to 250 subs

claim_audit.claim: On a 512 MB Pi that wall bites around ~100-150 subscribers - measure on the actual box before quoting a number
claim_audit.evidence: 2.39MB/sub implies ~125-165 subs from ~300-400MB usable on a 512MB box; DEMO correctly hedges 'measure on the actual box'

claim_audit.claim: B3 20 subscribers: 20 FIFOs, ~110 procs (~5 procs + ~2.4MB RAM per sub)
claim_audit.evidence: at 100 subs I saw 510 total procs (~5/sub) and 100 live FIFOs; consistent
0 CPU jiffies across the whole broker fleet over 4s with 30 idle subs

proof_assessment.notes: Not exercised in this challenge; the M0 chaos gate is covered by the prod-chaos-5000 pair. I re-ran flood_wedged.sh twice for the H4 regression: pass=4/0 both runs, max_descendants=19 (flat), drops 362/361, ~3s, no cold-start hang.
